title: Add --force to podman umount to force the unmounting of the rootfs
body:
podman umount will currently only unmount file system if not other
process is using it, otherwise the umount decrements the container
storage to indicate that the caller is no longer using the mount
point, once the count gets to 0, the file system is actually unmounted.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: BTW I also added a error if you try to umount an image that was not mounted.  Previously you could keep umounting and it would just print the CID, even if the container wasn't mounted.
LGTM aside from the comment from @edsantiago
LGTM other than the usage message. I like the new check for mountedness.
